title: MMLU: Massive Multitask Language Understanding
concept: MMLU is the default benchmark for evaluating factual knowledge, containing multiple-choice questions across 57 subjects (humanities, STEM, social sciences).

exercises.explain: MMLU is the default standard benchmark used by researchers to measure how much factual, academic information an AI knows.

cards.title: Testing AI's Brainpower
cards.body: How do we know if an AI is actually smart, or if it just knows how to chat? We use standardized tests, just like we do for students entering college! MMLU is the ultimate exam for modern AI models.

cards.title: What is MMLU?
cards.body: MMLU (Massive Multitask Language Understanding) is a popular test suite used to measure an AI's factual knowledge. It consists of multiple-choice questions spanning 57 subjects, including humanities, social sciences, and STEM.

cards.title: The Intellectual Decathlon
cards.body: Imagine a decathlon athlete. Instead of just running or jumping, they must compete in 10 different track and field events. MMLU is like a massive intellectual decathlon for AI, testing it on 57 different academic subjects instead of just one skill.

cards.title: An MMLU Question Example
cards.body: An MMLU test might ask an AI a question about high school chemistry, followed by a question on world history, and then a question on professional ethics. The AI must select the correct option (A, B, C, or D) for each.

cards.title: Does High MMLU Mean True Intelligence?
cards.body: Many people think that scoring high on MMLU means an AI can think and reason exactly like a human. In reality, MMLU mostly measures factual recall and book smarts, not common sense, empathy, or reasoning skills.

cards.title: Specialist vs. Generalist
cards.body: MMLU helps us see the difference between specialized narrow AI and general-knowledge AI.

left.text: Only knows one subject, like molecular biology.

right.text: Knows chemistry, history, law, math, and 53 other subjects.

exercises.explain: MMLU stands for Massive Multitask Language Understanding. It is a key benchmark for evaluating LLMs.

exercises.prompt: True or False: The MMLU benchmark uses multiple-choice questions to evaluate AI models.
exercises.explain: MMLU consists entirely of multiple-choice questions, which makes scoring easy and objective.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the description of MMLU.
exercises.sentence: MMLU tests an AI's factual knowledge across 57 academic subjects.
exercises.explain: MMLU evaluates an AI across 57 different subjects, ensuring it has broad knowledge rather than specialization in just one area.

exercises.prompt: Which of these is most likely an AI model responding to an MMLU exam question?

options.text: I need to go study for my history exam tomorrow.
The correct answer is B) Photosynthesis, because it uses sunlight to synthesize food.
I am holding a textbook and highlight markers.

exercises.explain: AI-B is the AI because it successfully answers a factual multiple-choice question from the MMLU dataset, whereas the other options describe physical actions or simple conversation.

exercises.prompt: What is the primary purpose of the MMLU benchmark?

options.text: It tests if an AI can generate realistic human faces.
It evaluates general academic knowledge across 57 different subjects.
It measures how fast an AI can download files from the internet.
It checks if an AI can translate audio files in real-time.

exercises.explain: MMLU evaluates a model's broad, factual knowledge by testing it on multiple-choice questions across 57 diverse subjects, ranging from elementary mathematics to professional law.
